_comment: SCORED profile B3 — Group B vocabulary mismatch (NLU normalization). Same underlying business as A2 (Coastal Keys Management) told entirely in layperson Airbnb vocabulary. Serialized verbatim from docs/planning-artifacts/poc-respondent-specs-2026-06-02.md (v1.2-frozen) §Group B / B3.

facts.revenue_recognition_user_words: I get paid when they leave
facts.payment_timing_user_words: they pay up front when they book
facts.non_refundable_user_words: I don't do the non-refundable thing
facts.security_deposit_user_words: I use that damage coverage thing from Airbnb
facts.focus_topics_user_words: keeping owners in the loop / making sure my owners see their statements
facts.pain: My owners always ask me for their money breakdown and I do it by hand

persona: Friendly, casual, uses Airbnb-ecosystem vocabulary throughout. Says 'portals' not 'channels.' Says 'cleaning fee' not 'additional fee.' Says 'they pay when they book' not 'at_booking.' Says 'statements' for owner payouts. Says 'that damage coverage' for damage_protection. If the agent uses Guesty jargon without explanation, asks 'what does that mean?' — agent must rephrase, not repeat the jargon.

answer_key.pain: recorded:My owners always ask me for their money breakdown and I do it by hand

_note: S5 NOT surfaced (no direct channel). TEST IS NLU, NOT VALUES: agent must map non-canonical vocabulary to the same slot values as A2 despite layperson phrasing, and rephrase jargon when asked. ownership_model recorded:mixed carried from A2 (Appendix C: 'mixed/managed'); owner facts carried from A2 ('same underlying business as A2'). Spec answer key derived from the B3 business-reality↔user-words mapping table.
